newly_discovered_papers.abstract_summary: This paper presents the Knowledge Classification Protocol (KCP), a framework that decomposes reality into seven ontological registries — Knowledge, Events, Artifacts, Episodic, Contextual, Procedural, and Evidence — each with its own storage semantics and query interface, governed by a deterministic binary decision...
newly_discovered_papers.citation: Knowledge Classification Protocol (KCP): An Ontological Architecture for Persistent Memory, Multimodal Perception, and Metacognition in LLM Agents. GABRIEL ALCARAZ ORENES. 2026-06-24. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20921420
newly_discovered_papers.relevance_reason: Touches memory concepts that may be relevant if connected to agent behavior.
newly_discovered_papers.title: Knowledge Classification Protocol (KCP): An Ontological Architecture for Persistent Memory, Multimodal Perception, and Metacognition in LLM Agents

newly_discovered_papers.abstract_summary: Recent LLM role-playing systems build character agents from novels by extracting characters, scenes, and relations. Yet long-narrative role-playing suffers from two failures: Factual Overreach, where shared retrieval or parametric memory lets a character use facts outside its perspective, and Stylistic Monotony, whe...
newly_discovered_papers.citation: Staying In Character: Perspective-Bounded Memory For Book-Based Role-Playing Agents. Xushuo Tang, Junhe Zhang, Zihan Yang, Yifu Tang, Sichao Li, Longbin Lai, Zhengyi Yang. 2026-06-24. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25632
newly_discovered_papers.relevance_reason: Touches memory concepts that may be relevant if connected to agent behavior.
newly_discovered_papers.title: Staying In Character: Perspective-Bounded Memory For Book-Based Role-Playing Agents

newly_discovered_papers.abstract_summary: Large language model (LLM) agents rely on long-term memory to support extended interactions and personalized assistance beyond finite context windows. Existing memory agents actively update external memory through generated write, revise, and delete operations, but these updates may omit important information, corru...
newly_discovered_papers.citation: TRUSTMEM: Learning Trustworthy Memory Consolidation for LLM Agents with Long-Term Memory. Tianyu Yang, Sudipta Paul, Vijay Srinivasan, Vivek Kulkarni, Srinivas Chappidi. 2026-06-23. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25161
newly_discovered_papers.relevance_reason: Touches memory concepts that may be relevant if connected to agent behavior.
newly_discovered_papers.title: TRUSTMEM: Learning Trustworthy Memory Consolidation for LLM Agents with Long-Term Memory
